name: STEMI — Septal
identifyBy: ST elevation ≥1mm in V1 and V2 (septal leads)
May extend to involve anterior territory (V1–V4)
Often associated with LBBB or RBBB (septal infarction disrupts conduction)
Reciprocal changes may be subtle — check inferior leads
Septal Q waves may develop (loss of septal depolarisation)

measurements.prInterval: May be prolonged or first-degree block

management: Consider bundled STEMI protocol — septal MI has high conduction block risk
IV access, monitor for AV block and complete heart block
If complete heart block develops — transcutaneous pacing ready
pearls: Septal STEMI frequently causes complete AV block — have pacing equipment ready
New RBBB in the context of acute chest pain = possible septal infarction
Isolated V1–V2 ST elevation may represent posterior STEMI mirror — check V7–V9
Septal infarcts often co-present with anterior infarcts (proximal LAD occlusion)
differentials: Brugada pattern (V1–V2 coved ST, no reciprocal changes)
